text:
Andy Murray,Rafael NadalandNovak Djokovicare all playing at the 2024Olympic Gamestennis tournament inParis, but when can you watch them?

Read on for the date, start time, fixtures, teams, format and schedule as the Big Three stars fight for gold – with Wimbledon winner Carlos Alcaraz also in action – although there is no world No.1 Jannik Sinner after he pulled out over tonsillitis.

British sporting icon Murray, 37, was hoping for a third gold medal in singles beforehe withdrew on Thursdayto focus on the men’s doubles with Dan Evans.

Murray will retire from tennis after the Olympics while Nadal, 38, is also featuring in the men’s doubles and is representing Spain next to SW19 champion Alcaraz.

Unlike Murray, Nadal is playing singles and could potentially face Djokovic, 37, in a blockbuster second-round clash after the draw was made on Thursday.

Djokovic is the only Big Three star to have never won a gold medal and he isdesperate to secure onefor Serbia in what is almost certain to be his last chance to do so given his age. It is also assumed that Nadal is playing his last Olympics.

Here’s absolutely everything you need to know about the Olympic tennis tournament and how you can watch Murray, Nadal and Djokovic in action…

The Olympic tennis schedule is packed into one week – running from July 27 to August 4.

Andy Murray isset to retire from tennisafter the Olympic Games in Paris and is only solely playing in the men’s doubles with his British compatriot Dan Evans.

The draw was made on Thursday, July 25 with Murray and Evans set to face Japan’s Taro Daniel and Kei Nishikori in their first-round doubles match.

Should they win that clash, Murray and Evans would then take on Belgian duo Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen or French pair Arthur Fils and Ugo Humbert.

The first-round men’s doubles matches will be played on Saturday, July 27 and Sunday, July 28 – and like the singles – they will take place from 11am or 6pm UK time.

Men’s Singles: Cameron Norrie, Jack Draper, Dan Evans

Women’s Singles: Katie Boulter

Men’s Doubles: Joe Salisbury & Neal Skupski, Dan Evans & Andy Murray

Women’s Doubles: Katie Boulter & Heather Watson

Mixed Doubles:Joe Salisbury & Heather Watson

It is almost certain Rafael Nadal is playing at the Olympics for the last time before his retirement from professional men’s tennis – if he doesn’t withdraw due to hisworrying new thigh injury.

Nadal wasrecently being namedon the US Open entry list and has delayed plans to hang up his racket – but he’ll surely be too old to feature at Los Angeles 2028.

There are64players in both the men’s singles and women’s singles draws.

The men’s and women’s doubles will each be32teams – and mixed doubles will be16teams.

Nadal is playing men’s singlesandmen’s doubles. The King of Clay’s first-round matches will be played on Saturday, July 27 or Sunday, July 28 from 11am or 6pm UK time.

Nadal faces Marton Fucsovics in his opening singles match and then would be projected to face Novak Djokovic in a colossal second-round showdown.

Men’s singles: Saturday, July 27 – Sunday, August 4

Men’s doubles: Saturday, July 27 – Saturday, August 3

Women’s singles: Saturday, July 27 – Saturday, August 3

Women’s doubles: Saturday, July 27 – Sunday, August 4

Mixed doubles: Monday, July 29 – Friday, August 2

In the men’s doubles, Nadal and Carlos Alcaraz get started against Argentine team Andres Molteni and Maximo Gonzalez in their first match.

‘I am very excited to be in my first Games and to be with Rafa,’ Alcaraz said. ‘We are going to give our best. I have less experience and I hope to be able to adapt quickly to playing doubles. I like to play it, but due to individual issues I have never been able to. I can learn everything from Rafa.’

Rafa Nadal’s potential path to winning a singles medal at the Paris Olympics:

Novak Djokovic bagged Serbia’s first Olympic tennis medal in 2008 when he clinched bronze but he hasn’t landed once since and is very much craving gold.

The 24-time Grand Slam champion is only playing in singles and decided not to enter the doubles tournament after his recentheartbreaking Wimbledon defeat.

Djokovic’s first-round match in the Olympic tennis singles will take place on Saturday, July 27 or Sunday, July 28 from 11am or 6pm UK time.

He faces Australia’s Matthew Ebden in his opener and then could be set to lock horns with his fellow Big Three tennis legend Rafael Nadal.

Novak Djokovic’s possible path to landing a singles medal at the Paris Olympics:

Here is the full schedule for the Olympic tennis tournament, which is being played on the Paris clay, usually home to the iconic French Open.

Russian and Belarusian tennis players will compete under the International Olympic Committee (IOC) flag.

This includes recent Wimbledon semi-finalist Daniil Medvedev, with the International Tennis Federation (ITF) confirming their stance in a June statement.

‘The International Olympic Committee and International Paralympic Committee will allow qualified and eligible athletes with Russian and Belarusian passports to compete in the Paris 2024 Olympic Games as Individual Neutral Athletes (“AIN”) and Paralympic Games as Neutral Paralympic Athletes (“NPA”),’ the ITF said.

‘The ITF confirms that tennis players from these nations will be allowed to enter the Olympic and Paralympic tennis competitions in an individual and neutral capacity if they comply with the IOC’s AIN and the IPC’s NPA principles. Neutral athletes will need to meet the selection and eligibility requirements as set out in the applicable ITF Regulations.’

There will be 32 teams in the both men’s and women’s doubles at the Olympic Games in Paris, with 16 in the mixed doubles.

The mixed doubles pairings were revealed on Wednesday, July 24.

Women’s doubles

Mixed doubles

title: When do Andy Murray, Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic play at the Olympics? | Metro News
description: Date, start times and full schedule for the 2024 Olympic Games tennis tournament in Paris for Andy Murray, Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic.
keywords: Sport,Tennis,Andy Murray,Dan Evans,Los Angeles,Novak Djokovic,Olympics,Rafael Nadal
